reasoning: Hamas's core ideology and operational strategy are heavily reliant on its armed wing, making it highly unlikely they would officially agree to disarm by December 2025. Even with the ongoing conflict and pressure, Hamas has continuously rejected demands to disarm. Offers or suggestions of disarmament from Hamas officials have always been highly conditional (e.g., creating a fully sovereign Palestinian state on 1967 borders), which Israel is currently unwilling to accept. Given the resolution criteria requires an official policy announcement without contingencies, the chance of a concrete, unconditional announcement of disarmament is extremely low.
